content.full_text:
s 
September 14, 1977 
Mr. John Giancello 
Fair Political Practices ConMission 
1100 "K" Street 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Dear Mr. Giancello: 
J 
I am sending you this letter to confirm 
our telephone conversations of September 9 and 12, 
1977, regarding the reportability of any non-monetary 
value attributable to the use of a volunteer's home 
for an evening campaign benefit party. 
All consumable 
items or expenses defrayed by the homeowner which are 
identifiable from general overhead are agreed to be 
reportable non-monetary contributions. 
This question 
addresses only the need to report a prorated amount 
for rent, taxes, insurance, etc. 
I also posed a question regarding reporting 
the use of telephones belonging to a San Diego business 
during non-business hours. 
The enterprise in question 
has made available to our Committee a bank of telephones 
for use by our volunteers for a telephonic get-out-the-
vote campaign. 
Again, any identifiable expenses attributable 
to making the telephones and/or office available to us 
which can be separated from the general overhead of the 
business will, of course, be reported. 
Your response to my inquiry in both of the 
above situations was that no non-monetary contribution 
need be reported for any pro rata share of the homeowner's 
rent, taxes, insurance, etc., or any of the business's 
general overhead. 
If this understanding does not agree 
with your recollection, please contact me immediately. 
flam 
StR~e)re 
/~ 
i 
yours, 
John M. Burns 
Treasurer

sections.question_synthetic: Whether non-monetary contributions must be reported for prorated amounts of homeowner expenses (rent, taxes, insurance) used for a campaign benefit party and for general overhead costs of a business providing telephone access for volunteer get-out-the-vote activities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advised that no non-monetary contribution need be reported for pro rata shares of homeowner rent, taxes, insurance, or business general overhead; only identifiable, separable expenses attributable to making resources available should be reported.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming letter from a requester confirming prior FPPC telephone advice, not a formal FPPC advice letter response. No formal Q/A sections present; contains paraphrased FPPC guidance within the body of the letter. Confidence reduced due to lack of official FPPC letterhead/response format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether non-monetary contributions must be reported for prorated amounts of homeowner expenses (rent, taxes, insurance) used for a campaign benefit party and for general overhead costs of a business providing telephone access for volunteer get-out-the-vote activities.

CONCLUSION: FPPC advised that no non-monetary contribution need be reported for pro rata shares of homeowner rent, taxes, insurance, or business general overhead; only identifiable, separable expenses attributable to making resources available should be reported.

All consumable items or expenses defrayed by the homeowner which are identifiable from general overhead are agreed to be reportable non-monetary contributions. This question addresses only the need to report a prorated amount for rent, taxes, insurance, etc. I also posed a question regarding reporting the use of telephones belonging to a San Diego business during non-business hours. The enterprise in question has made available to our Committee a bank of telephones for use by our volunteers for a telephonic get-out-the- vote campaign. Again, any identifiable expenses attributable to making the telephones and/or office available to us which can be separated from the general overhead of the business will, of course, be reported. Your response to my inquiry in both of the above situations was that no non-monetary contribution need be reported for any pro rata share of the homeowner's rent, taxes, insurance, etc., or any of the business's general overhead. If this understanding does not agree with your recollection, please contact me immediately. flam StR~e)re /~ i yours, John M. Burns Treasurer
embedding.summary: A committee treasurer confirms telephone conversations with FPPC staff regarding the reportability of non-monetary contributions for use of a volunteer's home and business telephones for campaign activities, and documents FPPC's guidance that general overhead and prorated fixed costs need not be reported.
